text:
There once was a wise old fox named Felix. He lived in a lush forest filled with colorful birds and deep pools where lotus blooms floated. Felix liked solitude but enjoyed telling stories to squirrels at evening. One day, a young squirrel named Pip insisted on hearing Felix tell him one of his famous tales.

"The Whispering Woods," Felix began. "'There once was a creature so shy it could hear its own whispers. This creature would hide during rainbows and peek out at moons. One night, it decided to venture out boldly.'"

Pip listened attentively. "Then?"

"Funny thing happened. It found a talking moth. 'Good evening,' the moth said."

"The owl!"

"No.' The creature smiled. 'A dream.' It dreamed of mighty rivers and big cities. Back home, stars twinkled brighter."

"Pip?"

"For once, nothing."

Suddenly, rustles through the leaves. A bear!

Pip whimpered happily. The fox smiled.
